title: Merging filters for a graphic processing unit
abstract: Techniques for merging multiple filters into a single GPU program is described. The system includes a filtering engine, which receives an input to apply a plurality of filters to a source image. The filtering engine identifies a first type of filter and a second type of filter from the input. The filtering engine identifies a supplemental transformation filter from the input, implements the supplemental transformation filter using a custom function to generate a color value at the source image and merges the first type of filter and the second type of filter based on the supplemental transformation filter. Finally, the filtering engine may apply the merged filter on the source image to generate a destination image.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer- implemented method comprising: receiving, using one or more computing devices, an input to apply a plurality of filters to a source image; identifying, using the one or more computing devices, a first type of filter from the input; identifying, using one or more computing devices, a second type of filter from the input; identifying, using the one or more computing devices, a supplemental transformation filter from the input; implementing, using one or more computing devices, the supplemental transformation filter using a custom function to generate a color value at the source image; merging, using the one or more computing devices, the first type of filter and the second type of filter based on the supplemental transformation filter to generate a merged filter; and applying, using the one or more computing devices, the merged filter on the source image to generate a destination image. 2. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the first type of filter is a color transformation filter that maps a first color of a first pixel from the source image to a second color in the destination image. 3. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the second type of filter is a coordinate transformation filter that maps at least one pixel location in the destination image to one or more different pixel locations in the source image. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 further comprising: determining, using the one or more computing devices, that the supplemental transformation filter is not identified in the received input; inserting, using the one or more computing devices, a substitute supplemental transformation filter into the received input; and merging, using the one or more computing devices, the first type of filter and the second type of filter based on the substitute supplemental transformation filter. 5. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the first type of filter is applied on the source image in forward order based on the supplemental transformation filter. 6. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the second type of filter is applied on the source image in reverse order based on the supplemental transformation filter. 7. The computer- implemented method of claim 1, wherein the supplemental transformation filter is based on a fragment shader and runs in a single pass. 8. A computer program product comprising a non-transitory computer useable medium including a computer readable program, wherein the computer readable program when executed on a computer causes the computer to: receive an input to apply a plurality of filters to a source image; identify a first type of filter from the input; identify a second type of filter from the input; identify a supplemental transformation filter from the input; implement the supplemental transformation filter using a custom function to generate a color value at the source image; merge the first type of filter and the second type of filter based on the supplemental transformation filter; and apply the merged filter on the source image to generate a destination image. 9. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the first type of filter is a color transformation filter that maps a first color of a first pixel from the source image to a second color in the destination image. 10. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the second type of filter is a coordinate transformation filter that maps at least one pixel location in a destination image to one or more different pixel locations in the source image. 11. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the computer readable program when executed on the computer also causes the computer to: 

 determine that the supplemental transformation filter is not identified in the received input; insert a substitute supplemental transformation filter into the received input; and merge the first type of filter and the second type of filter based on the substitute supplemental transformation filter. 12. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the first type of filter is applied on the source image in forward order based on the supplemental transformation filter. 13. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the second type of filter is applied on the source image in reverse order based on the supplemental transformation filter. 14. The computer program product of claim 8, wherein the supplemental transformation filter is based on a fragment shader and runs in a single pass. 15. A system comprising: one or more processors; a user interface module executable by the one or more processors configured to receive an input to apply a plurality of filters to a source image; and a filtering engine executable by the one or more processors configured to: identify a first type of filter from the input; identify a second type of filter from the input; identify a supplemental transformation filter from the input; implement the supplemental transformation filter using a custom function to generate a color value at the source image; merge the first type of filter and the second type of filter based on the supplemental transformation filter; and apply the merged filter on the source image to generate a destination image. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the filter engine comprises a color transformation module as the first type of filter for mapping a first color from the source image to a second color in the destination image. 17. The system of claim 15, wherein the filter engine comprises a coordinate transformation module as the second type of filter for mapping a first pixel location from the source image to a second pixel location in the destination image. 18. The system of claim 15, wherein the filter engine comprises a supplemental transformation module for merging the first type of filter and the second type of filter based on the substitute supplemental transformation filter, the supplemental transformation module determining whether a supplemental transformation filter is identified in the received input and using the identified filter or inserting a substitute supplemental transformation filter for merging the first type of filter and the second type of filter. 19. The system of claim 15, wherein the first type of filter is applied on the source image in forward order based on the supplemental transformation filter. 20. The system of claim 15, wherein the second type of filter is applied on the source image in reverse order based on the supplemental transformation filter. 21. The system of claim 15, wherein the supplemental transformation filter is based on a fragment shader and runs in a single pass.
